stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Vivian Suter is a Swiss-Argentine painter who works in Guatemala.
  • Her work has been exhibited in Europe, the United States, and Latin America, among other places.
  • Many of her paintings incorporate natural elements gathered from the forest around her studio.
  • Her painting Tintin’s Sister (2017) incorporates volcanic ash and wind-blown leaves.
  • Her painting Nisyros (2019) incorporates clay sediments and pine needles.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two works. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Tintin’s Sister (2017) uses volcanic ash and wind-blown leaves; Nisyros (2019), however, uses clay sediments and pine needles.
Like many of Suter’s paintings, both Tintin’s Sister and Nisyros incorporate natural elements gathered from the forest: Tintin’s Sister uses volcanic ash, and Nisyros uses clay sediments.
Suter’s work, which often incorporates natural elements, has been exhibited both within Europe and abroad.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It names both paintings and asserts a shared feature: both incorporate natural elements gathered from the forest.

Choice A emphasizes a difference (different materials). Choice B reports different years. Choice D describes Suter’s work generally without focusing on the two named paintings.
